metadata.name: UDIP RAGAS Evaluation Dataset
metadata.description: Test queries with ground truth for RAGAS faithfulness and relevancy evaluation

test_cases.query: What are the main benefits of using PageIndex over traditional vector-based RAG?
expected_contexts: PageIndex provides page-level traceability and reasoning-based hierarchical document indexing without requiring vector embeddings.
Unlike vector RAG, PageIndex uses tree search with LLM reasoning over document structure to return precise citations.
test_cases.ground_truth: PageIndex offers page-level traceability and reasoning-based hierarchical indexing without requiring vector embeddings, using tree search with LLM reasoning for precise citations.

test_cases.query: How does TurboQuant reduce VRAM usage during inference?
expected_contexts: TurboQuant uses asymmetric KV cache quantization with 3-4 bit precision, reducing memory by approximately 59% with only 0.86% perplexity loss.
Keys use higher precision (q8_0) than values (q4_0) because keys are more sensitive to quantization.
test_cases.ground_truth: TurboQuant reduces VRAM usage through asymmetric KV cache quantization at 3-4 bit precision, achieving approximately 59% memory reduction with minimal perplexity impact, with keys quantized higher than values.

test_cases.query: What is the three-tier model system and how are models assigned?
expected_contexts: T1 (Always Resident): Embedding and retrieval models like Qwen3-0.6B and Qwen3-1.7B using 0.5-1.2 GB VRAM.
T2 (Semi-Resident): Reasoning models like Qwen3-4B and Qwen3-8B using 2.5-5.5 GB VRAM, unload after TTL.
T3 (On-Demand): High-capability models like Qwen3-14B and Qwen3-30B-A3B using 8.5-18 GB VRAM, loaded only when needed.
test_cases.ground_truth: The three-tier model system consists of T1 (always resident, embedding/retrieval, 0.5-1.2GB), T2 (semi-resident, reasoning, 2.5-5.5GB), and T3 (on-demand, high-capability, 8.5-18GB), with models assigned based on query complexity and VRAM availability.

test_cases.query: How does the VRAM pressure management system work?
expected_contexts: GREEN (<70%): All tiers available, T3 loads on-demand.
YELLOW (70-85%): Downgrade T3 KV cache, reduce T2 context by 50%.
ORANGE (85-93%): Unload T3, route generation to T2, reduce T1 concurrency.
RED (>93%): Emergency unload T2, T1-only with 2K token truncation.
test_cases.ground_truth: VRAM pressure management uses four levels: GREEN (<70%, all tiers available), YELLOW (70-85%, downgrade T3 KV cache), ORANGE (85-93%, unload T3 and route to T2), RED (>93%, emergency T1-only mode with token truncation).

test_cases.query: What are the four retrieval pipeline modes and when should each be used?
expected_contexts: tree (default for single-doc): PageIndex hierarchical tree search with LLM reasoning, returns page-level citations.
hybrid (for multi-doc breadth): Vector similarity + keyword retrieval merged and ranked.
naive: Pure vector similarity search.
combined: Tree search for precision + vector search for recall, results merged and deduplicated.
test_cases.ground_truth: The four retrieval modes are: tree (single-doc, PageIndex hierarchical with citations), hybrid (multi-doc, vector+keyword), naive (pure vector), and combined (tree+vector merged).

test_cases.query: Can you explain how RecursiveMAS works in the solve pipeline?
expected_contexts: RecursiveMAS implements three patterns: Sequential (Planner-Critic-Solver), Deliberation (Reflector-ToolCaller loops), and Mixture (parallel domain-specialized agents).
Convergence detection uses embedding similarity between rounds, with compressed 200-token summaries transferred between agents.
test_cases.ground_truth: RecursiveMAS integrates three collaboration patterns into the solve pipeline: Sequential (planner-critic-solver with recursion rounds), Deliberation (reflector-toolcaller recursive loops), and Mixture (parallel domain-specialized agents with summarizer).

test_cases.query: What is ARA and how does it improve knowledge retrieval?
expected_contexts: ARA (Artifact-Based Retrieval Augmentation) compiles knowledge artifacts in three layers: Logic (claims, concepts, experiments), Solution (architecture, heuristics, constraints), and Trace (exploration DAG with dead-end preservation).
ARA Rigor Reviewer provides quality benchmarking that replaces RAGAS stubs for production readiness validation.
test_cases.ground_truth: ARA (Artifact-Based Retrieval Augmentation) improves knowledge retrieval by compiling documents into three layered artifacts (Logic, Solution, Trace) and providing rigorous quality review through the ARA Rigor Reviewer.

test_cases.query: How does the complexity scorer determine which model tier to use?
expected_contexts: Four weighted signals: Query Complexity (0.35), Document Size (0.25), Retrieved Chunk Count (0.15), Available VRAM (0.25).
Decision: score <0.3 → T1 (fast path), 0.3-0.6 → T2 (balanced), >0.6 → T3 (quality path).
test_cases.ground_truth: The complexity scorer uses four weighted signals (query complexity 0.35, document size 0.25, chunk count 0.15, VRAM 0.25) to route queries to T1 (<0.3), T2 (0.3-0.6), or T3 (>0.6).

test_cases.query: What is the performance target for PageIndex tree search latency?
expected_contexts: PageIndex tree generation: <60s for 100-page document.
PageIndex tree search: <5s per retrieval.
test_cases.ground_truth: The performance target for PageIndex tree search is under 5 seconds per retrieval, with tree generation under 60 seconds for a 100-page document.

test_cases.query: What token throughput is expected with TurboQuant on RTX 3090/4090?
expected_contexts: Token throughput (TurboQuant): ≥15 tok/s on RTX 3090/4090 at 8K+ context.
test_cases.ground_truth: TurboQuant targets at least 15 tokens per second on RTX 3090/4090 GPUs at 8K+ context lengths.

test_cases.query: Does UDIP require an internet connection to function?
expected_contexts: The application ingests documents and enables multi-modal AI interactions entirely on local hardware with no cloud dependencies.
Web search is explicitly opt-in. Core pipeline works fully offline. Requires SEARCH_API_KEY and enable_web_search:true per request.
test_cases.ground_truth: UDIP does not require an internet connection for core functionality. It operates entirely locally with no cloud dependencies. Web search is opt-in only.

expected_contexts: Single GPU (device index 0) — Multi-GPU deferred to v2.
test_cases.ground_truth: UDIP currently supports only a single GPU (device index 0). Multi-GPU support is deferred to version 2.
